text: The surgeon begins the robotic esophagectomy by identifying the dissection plane and cutting the azygos vein using scissors. The bronchiolatory branch is visible, and the surgeon removes the high larynx node. The mediastinal pleura is opened, revealing the bone character behind the azygos vein.

The surgeon divides the bronchial artery, visible alongside the vagus nerve. Care is taken to avoid dissecting too deeply to prevent damage to the thoracic duct. Some branches are coagulated using monofilament.

The surgeon uses bipolar coagulation to manage the upper branches, noting the presence of larger lymph nodes in this area. The major lower branch is divided using clips, with visible control over the surrounding structures.

The surgeon opens the mediastinal pleura over the upper thoracic esophagus, revealing the esophageal hiatus. Small lymph nodes are visible around the posterior part of the trachea and anterior part of the esophagus. The vagus nerve is also identified in this area.
